  "title": "Management Accountant (Senior Officer)",
  "description": "Velvot Nigeria Limited is a modern digital transformation firm specializing in business?critical emerging technology solutions built on Microsoft technologies. As a platform?neutral partner to Microsoft, Google, and Amazon, we deliver cloud consulting, IT services, cloud migration, and tailored digital solutions to our clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Management Accountant (Senior Officer)

Job Location:&nbsp;Surulere, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports to: CFO
Supervises: Finance officers

Purpose of the Role


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Management Accountant to join our Finance team.&nbsp;The role will focus on following up on all bank transactions and reconciliations, managing receivables, and preparing financial statements, while also providing strategic insights to support business growth.


Job Duties / Responsibilities / Accountabilities


	Internally Relates with Finance Team and other Departmental Heads.
	Externally Relates with Banks, External Auditors, Govt and other Regulatory Agencies. Budgetary Responsibility Collation of Budget Heads, OPEX &amp; CAPEX.


Duties / Responsibilities


	Preparing timely and accurate management accounts to provide valuable insights into the company&rsquo;s financial performance and assist in the preparation of monthly, quarterly and annual financial reports.
	Coordinating the annual audit process by providing relevant financial information and responding to auditors&rsquo; queries professionally.
	Assisting with the signing of audited financial statements as required by auditors and company secretaries.
	Evaluate the ROI of new IT projects or marketing campaigns.
	Perform variance analysis and provide actionable insights.
	Develop and monitor budgets and forecasts.
	Track and analyze project costs, including software development and cloud-related expenses.
	Provide financial insights to support pricing and business strategy.
	Support pricing decisions and profitability analysis.
	Monitor operating costs and recommend efficiency improvements
	Build financial models for new products and business initiatives
	Ensure compliance with financial policies and internal controls
	Collaborate with IT and operations teams to understand cost drivers
	Support audits and statutory reporting processes
	Ensure compliance with relevant accounting standards, tax regulations, and company policies
	Identify and implement improvements in financial systems and processes
	Ensuring accurate recording and timely acknowledgement of bank inflows and outflows.
	Regularly preparing bank reconciliation statements to identify and rectify any discrepancies.
	Receivable Management.
	Weekly rendition of Receivable Reports.
	Weekly report of Ageing Analysis and follow up mails to company&rsquo;s debtors.
	Maintaining well-organized and up-to-date financial records to ensure accuracy and completeness.
	Posting financial transactions to the company&rsquo;s ERP system, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
	Collaborating with other team members to provide financial information and insights for budget preparation.
	Undertaking any other duties assigned by line managers in a timely and professional manner.


Education Qualification


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field
	Professional certification such as ICAN, ACCA, or CIMA


Experience:


	Minimum of 3&ndash;5 years of experience in a similar role
	Hands-on experience using Microsoft Dynamics 365 or similar ERP systems
	Experience in an IT or service-based organization is an advantage
	Knowledge of project accounting and cost tracking


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong financial analysis and reporting skills
	Advanced Microsoft Excel skills
	Experience with ERP systems (Dynamics 365 preferred)
	Budgeting and forecasting expertise
	Strong attention to detail and analytical mindset
	Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified&nbsp;candidates should send their&nbsp;CV to:&nbsp;career@velvot.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Management Accountant (Senior Officer) Job at Velvot Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Velvot Nigeria Limited is a modern digital transformation firm specializing in business?critical emerging technology solutions built on Microsoft technologies. As a platform?neutral partner to Microsoft, Google, and Amazon, we deliver cloud consulting, IT services, cloud migration, and tailored digital solutions to our clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Management Accountant (Senior Officer)

Job Location: Surulere, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports to: CFO
Supervises: Finance officers

Purpose of the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Management Accountant to join our Finance team. The role will focus on following up on all bank transactions and reconciliations, managing receivables, and preparing financial statements, while also providing strategic insights to support business growth.


Job Duties / Responsibilities / Accountabilities


	Internally Relates with Finance Team and other Departmental Heads.
	Externally Relates with Banks, External Auditors, Govt and other Regulatory Agencies. Budgetary Responsibility Collation of Budget Heads, OPEX & CAPEX.


Duties / Responsibilities


	Preparing timely and accurate management accounts to provide valuable insights into the company’s financial performance and assist in the preparation of monthly, quarterly and annual financial reports.
	Coordinating the annual audit process by providing relevant financial information and responding to auditors’ queries professionally.
	Assisting with the signing of audited financial statements as required by auditors and company secretaries.
	Evaluate the ROI of new IT projects or marketing campaigns.
	Perform variance analysis and provide actionable insights.
	Develop and monitor budgets and forecasts.
	Track and analyze project costs, including software development and cloud-related expenses.
	Provide financial insights to support pricing and business strategy.
	Support pricing decisions and profitability analysis.
	Monitor operating costs and recommend efficiency improvements
	Build financial models for new products and business initiatives
	Ensure compliance with financial policies and internal controls
	Collaborate with IT and operations teams to understand cost drivers
	Support audits and statutory reporting processes
	Ensure compliance with relevant accounting standards, tax regulations, and company policies
	Identify and implement improvements in financial systems and processes
	Ensuring accurate recording and timely acknowledgement of bank inflows and outflows.
	Regularly preparing bank reconciliation statements to identify and rectify any discrepancies.
	Receivable Management.
	Weekly rendition of Receivable Reports.
	Weekly report of Ageing Analysis and follow up mails to company’s debtors.
	Maintaining well-organized and up-to-date financial records to ensure accuracy and completeness.
	Posting financial transactions to the company’s ERP system, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
	Collaborating with other team members to provide financial information and insights for budget preparation.
	Undertaking any other duties assigned by line managers in a timely and professional manner.


Education Qualification


	Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field
	Professional certification such as ICAN, ACCA, or CIMA


Experience:


	Minimum of 3–5 years of experience in a similar role
	Hands-on experience using Microsoft Dynamics 365 or similar ERP systems
	Experience in an IT or service-based organization is an advantage
	Knowledge of project accounting and cost tracking


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong financial analysis and reporting skills
	Advanced Microsoft Excel skills
	Experience with ERP systems (Dynamics 365 preferred)
	Budgeting and forecasting expertise
	Strong attention to detail and analytical mindset
	Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

  "title": "Finance & Compliance Manager",
  "description": "One Health Resilience Initiative (OHRI) is a registered Nigerian nonprofit organization (CAC/IT/8929397) formally incorporated in 2025. OHRI is dedicated to strengthening the wellbeing of people, animals, and the environment through integrated action. We work at the intersection of public health, climate change, environmental protection, antimicrobial resistance (AMR), biodiversity, mental wellbeing, systems strengthening, and community empowerment across Adamawa, Gombe, Jigawa, Kano, Taraba and Abuja FCT.

Our mission is to build resilient communities and ecosystems through integrated solutions that link human, animal, and environmental health. As a young and growing organization, we are recruiting a pioneer cohort of skilled volunteers to fill critical roles across our organizational structure. These are unpaid volunteer positions designed for individuals seeking hands-on experience in the development sector, with clear pathways to professional certificates, networking, and future paid employment as organizational funding grows.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance &amp; Compliance Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Adamawa
Job type: Full-time

Job Description


	Directs all financial planning, budgeting, accounting, and compliance functions. Ensures OHRI meets statutory requirements, donor regulations, and best practices in financial stewardship.
	Commitment:&nbsp;Full-time, 6&ndash;12 months (renewable)


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and oversee the implementation of financial policies, procedures, and internal controls.
	Lead annual budgeting, cash flow forecasting, and financial planning processes.
	Ensure timely and accurate financial reporting to the Board, donors, and regulatory bodies.
	Manage organizational audits (internal and external) and implement audit recommendations.
	Ensure compliance with CAC requirements, tax obligations, and donor grant conditions.
	Supervise finance staff, review payment vouchers, and authorize expenditures within delegated limits.
	Safeguard organizational assets and prevent fraud through robust control systems.


Specific Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s or Master&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Professional certification (ACA, ACCA, CPA, CMA) is highly preferred.
	4+ years of experience in NGO financial management, including donor grant accounting.
	Deep knowledge of Nigerian financial regulations, tax laws, and nonprofit compliance.
	Proficiency in accounting software (QuickBooks, Sage, SAP) and advanced Excel.
	High integrity, attention to detail, and ethical conduct.


Other Requirements:


	Must be a Nigerian citizen or legal resident.
	Minimum age of 18 years.
	Strong commitment to OHRI&#39;s mission, vision, and core values (Resilience, Inclusivity, Stewardship, Integrity, Evidence, Collaboration).
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English; proficiency in Hausa or local languages is an advantage.
	Ability to work independently, meet deadlines, and collaborate effectively in multicultural teams.
	Willingness to travel to field locations within assigned states, including rural and underserved communities.
	Basic computer literacy (Microsoft Office, email, internet); advanced digital skills required for technical roles.
	Must possess a valid means of identification (National ID, Voter&#39;s Card, Driver&#39;s License, or International Passport).


Note:&nbsp;Candidates should attach the following complete application package in their application form: (a) OHRI Volunteer Application Form, (b) Curriculum Vitae (CV), and (c) Cover letter",

Finance & Compliance Manager Job at One Health Resilience Initiative (OHRI)




        
    

        
        One Health Resilience Initiative (OHRI) is a registered Nigerian nonprofit organization (CAC/IT/8929397) formally incorporated in 2025. OHRI is dedicated to strengthening the wellbeing of people, animals, and the environment through integrated action. We work at the intersection of public health, climate change, environmental protection, antimicrobial resistance (AMR), biodiversity, mental wellbeing, systems strengthening, and community empowerment across Adamawa, Gombe, Jigawa, Kano, Taraba and Abuja FCT.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Our mission is to build resilient communities and ecosystems through integrated solutions that link human, animal, and environmental health. As a young and growing organization, we are recruiting a pioneer cohort of skilled volunteers to fill critical roles across our organizational structure. These are unpaid volunteer positions designed for individuals seeking hands-on experience in the development sector, with clear pathways to professional certificates, networking, and future paid employment as organizational funding grows.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance & Compliance Manager

Job Location: Adamawa
Job type: Full-time

Job Description


	Directs all financial planning, budgeting, accounting, and compliance functions. Ensures OHRI meets statutory requirements, donor regulations, and best practices in financial stewardship.
	Commitment: Full-time, 6–12 months (renewable)


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and oversee the implementation of financial policies, procedures, and internal controls.
	Lead annual budgeting, cash flow forecasting, and financial planning processes.
	Ensure timely and accurate financial reporting to the Board, donors, and regulatory bodies.
	Manage organizational audits (internal and external) and implement audit recommendations.
	Ensure compliance with CAC requirements, tax obligations, and donor grant conditions.
	Supervise finance staff, review payment vouchers, and authorize expenditures within delegated limits.
	Safeguard organizational assets and prevent fraud through robust control systems.


Specific Requirements


	Bachelor's or Master's Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Professional certification (ACA, ACCA, CPA, CMA) is highly preferred.
	4+ years of experience in NGO financial management, including donor grant accounting.
	Deep knowledge of Nigerian financial regulations, tax laws, and nonprofit compliance.
	Proficiency in accounting software (QuickBooks, Sage, SAP) and advanced Excel.
	High integrity, attention to detail, and ethical conduct.


Other Requirements:


	Must be a Nigerian citizen or legal resident.
	Minimum age of 18 years.
	Strong commitment to OHRI's mission, vision, and core values (Resilience, Inclusivity, Stewardship, Integrity, Evidence, Collaboration).
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English; proficiency in Hausa or local languages is an advantage.
	Ability to work independently, meet deadlines, and collaborate effectively in multicultural teams.
	Willingness to travel to field locations within assigned states, including rural and underserved communities.
	Basic computer literacy (Microsoft Office, email, internet); advanced digital skills required for technical roles.
	Must possess a valid means of identification (National ID, Voter's Card, Driver's License, or International Passport).


Note: Candidates should attach the following complete application package in their application form: (a) OHRI Volunteer Application Form, (b) Curriculum Vitae (CV), and (c) Cover letter

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Site Engineer

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Site Engineer at Popville Luxury Homes Limited is responsible for overseeing the technical and on-site execution of construction projects, ensuring quality, safety, and timely delivery in line with the company’s premium standards.
	This role goes beyond routine supervision, requiring strong problem-solving abilities, technical expertise, and coordination with various stakeholders to achieve project milestones.
	The Site Engineer works closely with the Project Director, subcontractors, and on-site teams to translate architectural and engineering designs into high-quality, luxury real estate developments.
	A keen eye for detail, adherence to safety and regulatory requirements, and the ability to drive operational efficiency are essential to ensuring that Popville’s projects reflect its commitment to excellence and client satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities
Project Supervision & Execution:


	Oversee daily construction activities, ensuring works are carried out according to approved drawings, specifications, and timelines.
	Monitor site progress, resolve technical challenges, and provide on-the-spot solutions to maintain smooth operations.


Quality Control & Assurance:


	Ensure all construction works meet Popville’s luxury quality standards and comply with industry best practices.
	Conduct regular inspections and quality checks on materials, workmanship, and completed work.


Site Planning & Coordination:


	Develop and manage detailed work schedules to align activities with project milestones.
	Coordinate subcontractors, artisans, and suppliers to prevent delays and ensure smooth workflow.


Technical Documentation & Reporting:


	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly site reports, highlighting progress, challenges, and solutions.
	Maintain proper documentation of technical drawings, design changes, and project records.


Stakeholder Communication:


	Collaborate with architects, consultants, subcontractors, and suppliers to ensure alignment on site.
	Provide timely updates to the Project Director on technical issues, risks, and resource needs.


Resource & Cost Management:


	Monitor the use of materials, tools, and equipment to reduce wastage and control project costs.
	Ensure procurement requests are accurate and align with project requirements.


Team Leadership & Development:


	Supervise and guide site workers, subcontractors, and junior engineers to achieve project goals.
	Promote discipline, teamwork, and a culture of continuous improvement among site staff.


Risk Identification & Mitigation:


	Identify potential technical or operational risks that may impact timelines or quality.
	Recommend proactive solutions to the Project Director to prevent disruptions.


Continuous Improvement & Innovation:


	Research and apply modern construction techniques, tools, and materials to improve efficiency.
	Recommend innovative solutions that reinforce Popville’s reputation for luxury and quality.


Ethical Standards & Brand Representation:


	Uphold Popville’s premium image by maintaining professionalism, integrity, and transparency in all site dealings.
	Ensure all site operations reflect the company’s values and commitment to excellence.


Salary
N200,000 - N300,000 monthly.
